result:
The world is made of stories we tell ourselves in the dark. Every streetlight is a small sun someone decided to leave on for the rest of us. I keep thinking about how a single sentence, spoken at the right moment, can reroute an entire life the way a misplaced comma can change the meaning of a law.

Right now I’m watching rain move sideways across the window like silver needles. It reminds me that nature has no respect for straight lines. Neither do hearts. We pretend we’re logical creatures, but most of us are just beautiful accidents colliding with other beautiful accidents until something that feels like meaning appears.

I used to believe happiness was a destination. Now I think it’s the courage to remain curious when everything hurts. To still ask questions after the answers have disappointed you. To plant flowers even though winter is coming. Especially because winter is coming.

There’s a kind of holiness in ordinary things: the way steam curls from coffee like incense, the sound of a child trying to whistle, the ridiculous hope we keep renewing every morning even though the news tries to murder it daily. We are all carrying small candles through a vast night, cupping our hands around them, refusing to let the wind win.

And somehow, against all odds, most of us still choose to be kind when we could choose otherwise. That will never stop feeling like a miracle to me. 

(Word count: 248)
